result:
## Summary

The root problem: this SaaS mid-pivoted from "Lumina" (an AI meeting assistant) to **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool), but only the blog and metadata got updated — the homepage, and the (missing) Features/Pricing pages, still marketed the old meeting-assistant product. Every single query in GSC is about InboxZap/email cleanup, so anyone clicking through from search landed on a page about a completely different product. That mismatch, plus several technical gaps, is the most plausible explanation for why 12 blog posts pull ~14K impressions at position 40-60 with near-zero clicks. Fixed:

- **Brand/product mismatch**: rewrote the homepage, nav, footer, and structured data around InboxZap; built out `/features` and `/pricing` (previously missing entirely from source, 404-bound).
- **Broken blog index**: was client-side fetching a nonexistent `/api/posts` (always showed a spinner or error to users and crawlers). Rebuilt as a server-rendered page with real, crawlable links.
- **Missing blog post pages**: reconstructed all 12 posts as a proper `app/blog/[slug]` route with per-post meta descriptions, canonical, OG/Twitter tags, and `BlogPosting`/`FAQPage` JSON-LD — none of which existed before.
- **Keyword cannibalization**: post-3 and post-7 both targeted "bulk delete emails" near-identically. Merged into one comprehensive guide at `/blog/post-3` with a 301 from `/blog/post-7`.
- **Sitemap**: was missing every blog post (left as a TODO). Now includes all 11 posts plus Features/Pricing.
- **Dead FAQ redirect**: `/faq` redirected to `/#faq`, but the homepage had no FAQ section. Added one, with schema.
- **Internal linking**: added related-posts links across all posts and CTAs from blog → pricing.

Verified with a full `npm install && npm run build && next start`: build succeeds, all 11 static post pages generate, redirects return 308 correctly, and metadata/schema render as expected on every page.

One unrelated item worth flagging: `npm install` reported next@14.2.5 has a known security vulnerability (patch available). I left the pinned version alone since upgrading was out of scope here, but you may want to schedule that separately.
